name: Avilés and Candás Loop
name_original: Embalse de la Granda, Cndás y Mirador de La Formiga – circular desde Avilés
description: This moderate 23.1-mile road cycling route from Avilés explores the Asturian coast, Embalse de la Granda, and Mirador de La Formiga.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Avilés offer a diverse landscape for cyclists, blending urban areas with scenic coastal and inland natural environments. The region features varied terrain, including the Cantabrian coastline with its beaches and coves, the Avilés estuary, and gentle inland hills. Many routes utilize paved surfaces, making them suitable for road cycling across different fitness levels. This area provides a mix of flat sections along the estuary and more challenging climbs into the surrounding hills. Best road cycling routes around Avilés The most popular road cycling route is Avilés and Candás Loop, a 23.1 miles (37.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers coastal views and connects two charming Asturian towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de San Martín – Santa María del Mar Beach loop from Villalegre, a moderate 33.0 miles (53.1 km) path. This route features a mix of inland and coastal scenery, leading to a popular beach. Local road cyclists also love the San Juan de Nieva Lighthouse – Playa de Xagó loop from Villalegre, a 19.6 miles (31.5 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Road cycling around Avilés is defined by coastal paths, estuary routes, and inland hills, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Avilés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 3,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Avilés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes can I find around Avilés?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes available around Avilés on komoot, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Avilés?
A: Road cycling in Avilés offers a diverse landscape, blending urban areas with scenic coastal and inland natural environments. You'll find routes along the Cantabrian coastline, paths through the Avilés estuary, and more challenging climbs into the surrounding hills. Most routes feature paved surfaces suitable for road bikes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Avilés?
A: Yes, Avilés offers routes for all skill levels. While many routes feature moderate to difficult sections, there are 8 easy routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40012555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro de Avilés – San Juan de Nieva Lighthouse loop from Llaranes&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 23.3 km ride with minimal elevation gain, perfect for those starting out.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the Avilés area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Avilés are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070374943/aviles-and-candas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Avilés and Candás Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de Nieva Lighthouse – Playa de Xagó loop from Villalegre&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic round trips.

Q: What are some scenic attractions or landmarks I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes around Avilés offer numerous scenic points. You can cycle past the dramatic cliffs and panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4016988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Peñas Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5039958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María del Mar Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the peaceful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrera Park 🌳 Avilés&lt;/a&gt;. The estuary paths also feature cultural elements like the San Sebastian Bridge and various sculptures.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes around 50 km in length?
A: Yes, there are several road cycling routes around 50 km. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24648559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Martín – Santa María del Mar Beach loop from Villalegre&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 53.1 km. This moderate route combines inland and coastal scenery, making for an enjoyable half-day ride.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Avilés?
A: The road cycling routes in Avilés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 27 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from coastal views to inland hills, and the well-maintained paved surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Avilés?
A: The region benefits from a mild climate, making road cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal rides, though it can be warmer. Winter cycling is possible, but it's advisable to check weather conditions as the Cantabrian coast can experience rain.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Avilés?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Avilés offers 34 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40375347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Martín – Bridge over the Nalón River loop from Villalegre&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 81.3 km route with nearly 1000 meters of elevation gain, requiring very good fitness.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views and connect to charming towns?
A: Many routes in Avilés are designed to showcase the stunning Cantabrian coastline and connect to picturesque towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070374943/aviles-and-candas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Avilés and Candás Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering beautiful sea views and linking two charming Asturian coastal communities.

Q: Are there any urban green spaces or parks accessible by road bike in Avilés?
A: Within Avilés itself, you can find peaceful urban green spaces like Parque de Ferrera and Parque del Muelle, which are great for leisurely strolls or short rides. The Parque de las Meanas, located along the Ría de Avilés, also features scenic paths suitable for cycling.
tips: Near the San Zabornín station we find ourselves rolling along this beautiful rural road. A luxury for road bike lovers.
Beautiful viewpoint to the beach and port of Candás. An ideal place if we want to take a break while enjoying the views. Access is by paved road.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a diverse and often challenging terrain typical of Asturias. You can expect a mix of rolling hills and some significant climbs, especially when approaching viewpoints like the Mirador de La Formiga. The roads are generally paved, suitable for racebikes, and will take you through coastal areas, green valleys, and past the serene Embalse de la Granda.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route from Avilés?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Avilés, you would typically look for parking within the city. Avilés offers various public parking options, both street parking and garages. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability, especially during peak times.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty grade, approximately 37 km distance, and 400 meters of elevation gain, this route is best suited for cyclists with some experience. While not extremely difficult, the presence of rolling hills and potential climbs means beginners might find it challenging. It's a good step up for those looking to advance beyond flat, easy rides.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Asturias, including this route, is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is typically milder, with pleasant temperatures and less extreme heat or cold, making for more comfortable cycling conditions. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially warmer temperatures.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route is designed to showcase some beautiful spots. You'll experience the scenic Cardo–Candás Road, and a major highlight is the View of Candás Beach and Harbor from Mirador de La Formiga, which offers panoramic vistas of the Cantabrian Sea and the charming coastal town.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is on public roads and generally accessible, road cycling with dogs typically requires them to be able to keep pace safely, often in a trailer or basket. If you plan to stop in towns like Candás, local regulations for dogs in public spaces and establishments should be observed. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort when cycling.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and passes through natural areas that are freely accessible. However, always respect local traffic laws and environmental regulations.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: This route offers a remarkable variety of landscapes. You'll start from the city of Avilés, transition to serene reservoir scenery around Embalse de la Granda, and then enjoy stunning coastal views as you approach and pass through the charming town of Candás. The Mirador de La Formiga provides expansive views combining both coastline and inland natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several populated areas where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops. Candás, in particular, is a coastal town known for its gastronomy and offers numerous options for refreshments and meals. Avilés, your starting point, also has a wide array of choices.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it includes parts of the Mtb Northern Route (Asturia) and the Northern Route (Asturia), as well as various segments of local 'Senda ciclista' (cycle paths) and 'senda peatonal' (pedestrian paths) in the area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained racebike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a phone for navigation and emergencies. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also advisable, especially on sunny days.

waypoints.name: View of Candás Beach and Harbor from Mirador de La Formiga
